refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall provide an integrated performance requirement derivation, recording, and management system that translates worksite mission context (site conditions, operational policies, safety constraints) into documented performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability), maintains a complete audit trail of baseline establishment and calibration updates, and ensures traceability from worksite inputs through performance targets to system design parameters.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is outcome-focused but lacks explicit mention of traceability, audit trail, and the bidirectional linkage between worksite context and performance targets. Refinement clarifies that this is a closed-loop system requirement with documentation and historical management as first-class concerns, not afterthoughts. High safety criticality demands explicit traceability and change control.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verification shall confirm: (1) worksite mission context is captured in a structured format; (2) performance targets are derived via documented method traceable to mission inputs; (3) baseline and calibration updates are recorded with timestamp, actor, and rationale; (4) traceability matrix links worksite inputs → performance targets → design parameters; (5) system prevents orphaned or undocumented performance targets.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Capture and structure worksite mission context (site conditions, operational policies, safety constraints) as input to performance derivation.
CR-NGHE-005.2: Derive performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability) from worksite mission context using a documented, repeatable method.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Record and maintain a complete audit trail of performance target baselines and calibration updates with metadata (timestamp, actor, rationale).
CR-NGHE-005.4: Establish and maintain traceability from worksite inputs through performance targets to system design parameters.
CR-NGHE-005.5: Provide query and reporting capability to retrieve performance targets and their derivation history for compliance and safety review.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized performance management database with structured schema for mission context, derived targets, and audit trail; coupled with a derivation engine that applies documented rules/algorithms to transform mission inputs into performance targets; integrated with design parameter traceability module.

DP-B: Distributed performance data collection across Vehicle and Teleoperation modules with eventual consistency synchronization to a central Safety Services repository; each module records its own performance observations and derives local targets, with periodic reconciliation.

DP-C: Manual performance requirement derivation process supported by templates and checklists; Site Manager and Safety Manager collaborate to define targets offline; system records final targets and change history via document versioning.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Centralized performance management database with structured schema for mission context, derived targets, and audit trail; coupled with a derivation engine that applies documented rules/algorithms to transform mission inputs into performance targets; integrated with design parameter traceability module.

potential_sources: DP-A couples performance derivation logic to the database schema; changes to derivation rules require schema updates. Mitigation: use a rule engine with versioned rule sets, decoupling rule logic from schema.

DP-A couples traceability to the derivation engine; if traceability requirements change, derivation logic may need revision. Mitigation: implement traceability as a separate concern (audit log) orthogonal to derivation.

DP-A couples mission context capture to the derivation engine; if mission context structure changes, derivation rules may break. Mitigation: use a flexible schema (e.g., JSON) and version mission context inputs.
coupling_check.notes: DP-A exhibits moderate coupling between derivation logic and data schema, but this is acceptable because both are owned by the same module (NGHE Solution or Safety Services). Coupling is internal and manageable. DP-B introduces distributed coupling across Vehicle and Teleoperation, increasing synchronization complexity and risk of inconsistency; rejected. DP-C introduces manual coupling to human processes, reducing repeatability and auditability; rejected for high safety criticality.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Worksite mission context can be captured in a structured, machine-readable format (e.g., JSON schema). Risk: If worksite inputs are highly unstructured or context-dependent, derivation may require human interpretation, reducing automation and auditability.

Assumption: Performance derivation rules are deterministic and can be documented. Risk: If derivation involves subjective judgment or domain expertise not easily codified, the system may produce inconsistent or non-reproducible targets.

Assumption: Design parameters are known and can be linked to performance targets. Risk: If design parameters are not yet finalized or are subject to change, traceability may become stale or incorrect.
Assumption: The system has access to historical baseline and calibration data. Risk: If historical data is incomplete or unreliable, audit trail may not be trustworthy.

Assumption: Safety criticality is managed through audit trail and traceability, not through real-time performance monitoring. Risk: If real-time performance deviation is a safety concern, this DP may be insufficient without coupling to a real-time monitoring system.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-A is sound but should be refined to explicitly separate concerns: (1) Mission Context Capture (structured input schema), (2) Derivation Engine (rule-based transformation with versioned rules), (3) Audit Trail (immutable log of baselines and updates), (4) Traceability Module (bidirectional links to design parameters). This separation reduces coupling and improves testability. Additionally, define a fallback or escalation path for cases where derivation rules cannot be applied (e.g., novel mission contexts), to ensure safety criticality is not compromised.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module owns the end-to-end performance requirement derivation, recording, and management system. This includes the centralized performance management database, derivation engine, audit trail, and traceability module. The module integrates mission context inputs from worksite stakeholders (Site Manager, Safety Manager) and provides performance targets and traceability reports to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives worksite mission context (site conditions, operational policies, safety constraints) from Site Manager and Safety Manager via Tasking interface. This includes mission objectives, environmental constraints, and safety requirements.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution outputs derived performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability) and traceability metadata via Telemetry interface to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules for operational guidance and monitoring.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution exchanges safety-critical information with Safety Services: receives safety constraints and policies (in), outputs performance targets with safety implications and audit trail for compliance review (out).

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural owner of system-level performance requirement management. The derivation engine, database, and traceability logic are core to the solution architecture and should not be distributed. Minimal diffusion: only external interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert) cross module boundaries. High containment score reflects strong cohesion of performance management concerns within this module.

Vehicle module is responsible for executing performance targets, not deriving them. Placing derivation logic in Vehicle would couple vehicle-specific design to system-level performance management, reducing reusability and increasing maintenance burden. High diffusion score reflects that performance derivation is orthogonal to vehicle control and actuation.

Teleoperation module is responsible for remote operation and control, not performance requirement management. Placing derivation logic in Teleoperation would couple tele-operator interface to system-level performance management, reducing clarity of responsibilities. High diffusion score reflects that performance derivation is orthogonal to teleoperation control.

Safety Services could own performance requirement derivation if the focus is on safety-critical performance targets (e.g., availability, fail-safe modes). However, performance derivation also includes non-safety concerns (e.g., energy efficiency, cycle time), which are outside Safety Services scope. Moderate containment score reflects partial alignment; recommend NGHE Solution as primary owner with Safety Services as stakeholder and reviewer.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the optimal owner because: (1) performance requirement derivation is a system-level concern that spans multiple operational domains (productivity, effectiveness, safety); (2) the derivation engine and database are core to the solution architecture and should not be replicated across Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services; (3) external interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert) provide clear boundaries for information flow; (4) ownership by NGHE Solution ensures single source of truth for performance targets and audit trail, critical for high safety criticality.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution module may become a bottleneck if performance derivation is computationally expensive or if mission context updates are frequent. Mitigation: design derivation engine for scalability (e.g., caching, incremental updates, asynchronous processing).

Risk: NGHE Solution may lack domain expertise in vehicle-specific or teleoperation-specific performance optimization. Mitigation: establish feedback loops from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to refine derivation rules; involve domain experts in rule definition.

Risk: NGHE Solution may not have real-time access to worksite mission context if Site Manager and Safety Manager are offline or if network connectivity is poor. Mitigation: implement local caching and offline derivation capability; synchronize when connectivity is restored.

Risk: Traceability to design parameters may become stale if design parameters change without updating the traceability module. Mitigation: implement change control process that requires traceability updates whenever design parameters are modified.
Risk: Audit trail may grow unbounded, impacting database performance and storage. Mitigation: implement retention policies and archival strategies; provide query optimization for historical data.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: NGHE Solution is the correct owner. Recommend refining the module scope to explicitly include: (1) Performance Requirement Derivation Engine (rule-based transformation), (2) Performance Management Database (structured schema for mission context, targets, metadata), (3) Audit Trail and Change Control (immutable log), (4) Traceability Module (bidirectional links to design parameters). Establish clear interfaces with Safety Services for safety-critical performance review and with Vehicle/Teleoperation for performance target dissemination. Define escalation path for novel mission contexts that cannot be automatically derived.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-A (centralized performance management database with derivation engine and traceability) directly addresses all aspects of the refined FR: (1) derives performance targets from worksite mission context, (2) records and maintains audit trail of baselines and calibration updates, (3) establishes traceability from worksite inputs to design parameters. The DP is solution-agnostic (does not prescribe specific technologies) and is appropriate for the level of abstraction. No FR-DP mismatch detected.

diffusion_risks: Risk: If derivation rules are not centralized in NGHE Solution, they may be duplicated across Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, leading to inconsistency and maintenance burden. Mitigation: enforce single-source-of-truth principle; all derivation logic resides in NGHE Solution.

Risk: If audit trail is distributed across multiple modules, traceability may be incomplete or fragmented. Mitigation: centralize audit trail in NGHE Solution; other modules log changes via SafetyAlert interface.

Risk: If performance targets are cached locally in Vehicle or Teleoperation modules, they may become stale if NGHE Solution updates them. Mitigation: implement cache invalidation strategy; Vehicle and Teleoperation modules query NGHE Solution for current targets via Telemetry interface.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution provides strong containment for performance derivation, database, and traceability logic. External interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert) are well-defined and minimize diffusion. No evidence of logic leakage to other modules. Containment hypothesis is validated.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of CR-NGHE-005 is feasible within NGHE Solution module: (1) worksite mission context capture can be verified by inspecting Tasking interface and database schema; (2) performance target derivation can be verified by testing derivation engine with representative mission contexts; (3) audit trail can be verified by inspecting database logs and change history; (4) traceability can be verified by querying traceability module and validating links to design parameters; (5) query and reporting capability can be verified by executing representative queries and inspecting output.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of CR-NGHE-005 to NGHE Solution is clear and unambiguous. Sub-FRs (CR-NGHE-005.1 through CR-NGHE-005.5) are all allocated to NGHE Solution with clear ownership. No ambiguity regarding which module is responsible for each sub-FR. Interfaces with Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are clearly defined.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-A to explicitly separate concerns: Mission Context Capture, Derivation Engine, Audit Trail, Traceability Module. This improves clarity and testability.
Define fallback/escalation path for novel mission contexts that cannot be automatically derived, to ensure safety criticality is not compromised.
Establish change control process that requires traceability updates whenever design parameters are modified.
Define retention and archival policies for audit trail to manage database growth.
Establish feedback loops from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to refine derivation rules based on operational experience.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-005 is not atomic and requires further decomposition. The refined FR identifies five sub-FRs (CR-NGHE-005.1 through CR-NGHE-005.5) that address distinct concerns: mission context capture, performance derivation, audit trail, traceability, and reporting. Each sub-FR is a candidate for the next level of functional decomposition. The selected DP (centralized performance management system) is sound and well-aligned with the FR, but the IM assignment (NGHE Solution) is at a high level of abstraction and requires further refinement into lower-level modules or components. Proceeding to the next decomposition level will clarify the internal architecture of the performance management system and establish detailed design parameters for each sub-FR.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Capture and structure worksite mission context (site conditions, operational policies, safety constraints) as input to performance derivation.
CR-NGHE-005.2: Derive performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability) from worksite mission context using a documented, repeatable method.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Record and maintain a complete audit trail of performance target baselines and calibration updates with metadata (timestamp, actor, rationale).
CR-NGHE-005.4: Establish and maintain traceability from worksite inputs through performance targets to system design parameters.
CR-NGHE-005.5: Provide query and reporting capability to retrieve performance targets and their derivation history for compliance and safety review.

assumptions: Worksite mission context can be captured in a structured, machine-readable format (e.g., JSON schema).
Performance derivation rules are deterministic and can be documented.
Design parameters are known and can be linked to performance targets.
The system has access to historical baseline and calibration data.
Safety criticality is managed through audit trail and traceability, not through real-time performance monitoring.
NGHE Solution module has sufficient computational resources to support performance derivation and database operations.
Worksite stakeholders (Site Manager, Safety Manager) can provide mission context in a timely manner.
Vehicle and Teleoperation modules can consume performance targets via Telemetry interface without modification.
open_risks: If worksite inputs are highly unstructured or context-dependent, derivation may require human interpretation, reducing automation and auditability.
If derivation involves subjective judgment or domain expertise not easily codified, the system may produce inconsistent or non-reproducible targets.
If design parameters are not yet finalized or are subject to change, traceability may become stale or incorrect.
If historical data is incomplete or unreliable, audit trail may not be trustworthy.
If real-time performance deviation is a safety concern, this DP may be insufficient without coupling to a real-time monitoring system.
If performance derivation is computationally expensive or if mission context updates are frequent, NGHE Solution may become a bottleneck.
If NGHE Solution lacks domain expertise in vehicle-specific or teleoperation-specific performance optimization, derived targets may be suboptimal.
If worksite mission context is not available in real-time (e.g., Site Manager offline), performance derivation may be delayed.
If design parameters change without updating the traceability module, traceability may become stale.
If audit trail grows unbounded, database performance and storage may be impacted.
evidence_needed: Worksite mission context schema and examples (to validate assumption that context can be structured).
Performance derivation rules and algorithms (to validate assumption that rules are deterministic and documented).
Design parameter catalog and traceability links (to validate assumption that design parameters are known and linkable).
Historical baseline and calibration data samples (to validate assumption that historical data is available and reliable).
Real-time performance monitoring requirements (to clarify whether real-time monitoring is a safety concern).
Computational performance analysis of derivation engine (to validate assumption that NGHE Solution can handle expected workload).
Domain expertise assessment for Vehicle and Teleoperation modules (to identify gaps in performance optimization knowledge).
Network connectivity and offline capability requirements (to clarify whether offline derivation is needed).
Change control process for design parameters (to establish traceability update procedures).
Database growth projections and retention policies (to plan for audit trail management).
